intro: Humboldtia crenata, also known as the 'Kannada' or 'Indian Cork Tree', is a tropical tree native to the Western Ghats region in India, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin ailments, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia crenata include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
